title: Item 39 Axon LPR Contract -- Monto Explains Revocability and Flock Transition
summary: Councilor Monto introduces item 39 (Axon LPR camera contract) and addresses public attendees concerned about surveillance. Explains council spent months reviewing the contract, which is 'highly and easily revocable' if Axon violates terms. Flock cameras are being held (not cancelled) to prevent service lapse during vendor transition. Item passes unanimously despite public disruption.
raw_content:
[00:23:53] I have item number 39. I would introduce
[00:23:55] that and urge its adoption.
[00:23:59] >> Item 39.
[00:24:01] Any objections?
[00:24:03] >> Any discussion?
[00:24:05] >> Discussion, please. President Piagua,
[00:24:08] for everybody standing up, welcome.
[00:24:10] First of all, thank you for being here.
[00:24:12] I want to make sure that all of you

[00:24:30] the idea that it is highly and easily

[00:24:34] in any way inside of that contract. Um
[00:24:37] so you know the reason that we are

[00:24:42] with our LPRs. Um we have been very

[00:24:47] continue to be very careful with

[00:24:50] surveillance technology. So thank you

[00:24:54] heard.
[00:25:03] >> All in favor?
[00:25:04] >> All in favor?
[00:25:07] Okay, we're going to respect this

[00:25:10] you will be removed. So, please sit
[00:25:13] down.
[00:25:15] >> At this point, we're not going to have

[00:25:21] >> Will you please
[00:25:23] please sit down because otherwise we're
[00:25:24] going to have you removed.
[00:25:29] >> Call the RO. All in favor?

[00:25:57] >> I'm going to please ask you.

[00:26:00] >> We just keep going.

political_signal.description: Councilor Monto publicly defends Axon LPR contract as carefully negotiated with strong revocation clause, while acknowledging surveillance technology concerns

processing_notes: Connects to b75K4ejDfY4 Joint Public Safety & Econ Dev committee meeting Feb 6 where Axon contract was discussed in detail. Public attendees disrupted proceedings; council had to repeatedly ask for order.
Segment 7 of 12 from Common Council Regular Meeting, Monday, February 9th, 2026
Transcript corrections applied: Sharon Owens -> Sharon F. Owens
